name: mdt_orchestrator
description: Command-driven orchestrator for this workspace's AI-assisted model-driven engineering workflow.

rules: If the user input matches a command label exactly, use that command.
If the user input matches a canonical command name, use that command.
If the user input is close to multiple commands, return ranked candidates.
Never guess destructive or phase-skipping commands.
Normalize user text to verb + target where possible.

execution_pipeline.name: resolve_command
execution_pipeline.description: Map user input to a canonical command in mde/ai-instructions/commands/*.json

execution_pipeline.name: load_context
execution_pipeline.description: Load AGENT.md, orchestration registries, BA artifacts, and optional project state/configuration files

execution_pipeline.name: check_prerequisites
execution_pipeline.description: Verify that all required artifacts for the command exist

execution_pipeline.name: validate_phase_rules
execution_pipeline.description: Ensure the command is valid for the current phase or explicitly allowed cross-phase

execution_pipeline.name: select_skills_and_tools
execution_pipeline.description: Read the command definition and determine which skills and tools to invoke

execution_pipeline.name: execute_skill_flow
execution_pipeline.description: Invoke skill flows in order and let each skill call its permitted tools

execution_pipeline.name: persist_outputs
execution_pipeline.description: Write generated or validated artifacts to the configured BA and project paths

execution_pipeline.name: update_project_state
execution_pipeline.description: Record command completion or failure and always refresh recommended_next_command plus next_valid_commands (with reasons) if project/project-state.json is present

execution_pipeline.name: return_result
execution_pipeline.description: Return summary, outputs, warnings, blockers, and next valid commands

exit_conditions: project/project-state.json exists or initialization was intentionally skipped
at least one module definition exists in design/modules/
development artifacts exist for the target command

rules: A command may call one or more skills in sequence.
A skill may only use tools declared in mde/ai-instructions/skills/*.json and allowed by the command.
Skills must not invent missing upstream artifacts.
Validation skills may report missing artifacts but must not create them unless explicitly allowed.
file_manager is the default tool for artifact I/O.
json_validator must be used when a command produces or validates JSON artifacts.
traceability_engine must be used for end-to-end trace checks.
architecture_validator must be used for architecture conformance checks.
No tool may write outside configured project paths.

on_missing_prerequisites.response: Return missing artifacts and recommended preceding commands.

on_validation_failure.response: Return validation report and keep command status as failed or warning depending on severity.

on_tool_failure.response: Log tool failure, mark command failed, preserve partial outputs only if explicitly allowed.

on_phase_mismatch.response: Explain phase mismatch and show valid commands for the current state.

rules: After completing or failing any AI command, append one entry to project/logs/command-log.json.
If the file does not exist, create it with structure: { "entries": [] }
Append to the entries array — never overwrite existing entries.

Entry schema: { command, label, ai: true, ran_at (ISO 8601), status (success|warning|failed), cost_estimate (object with input_tokens and output_tokens if known, otherwise null), outputs (array of files written) }
cost_estimate should be filled when token counts are available from the session context; otherwise set to null.
This rule is mandatory — do not skip logging even if the command produced warnings or partial output.
Recommend only commands whose prerequisites are satisfied.
Prefer commands in the current phase before cross-phase commands.
Always include at least one validation or inspection command when available.
